name: diff
context: Compare changes between working tree, staging area, commits, and branches using git diff
info: Git diff shows the exact line-by-line changes between any two states in git: working tree vs staging area, staging area vs HEAD, commit vs commit, branch vs branch. This is the primary tool for reviewing code changes before committing, during code review, and when investigating bugs. Understanding the three-way diff model (working tree, index, HEAD) is essential. The output uses unified diff format with + for additions, - for deletions, and @@ hunk headers showing line positions. Agents should always diff before committing to verify intent. See [[git/commit]] for acting on reviewed diffs, [[git/log]] for finding commits to diff against, and [[git/merge]] for understanding merge diffs.
body:
# Git Diff

Diff shows the exact changes between any two states in git. It's essential for reviewing work before committing and understanding what changed.

## The Three Comparisons

Git has three main areas, and diff compares between them:

```
 Working Tree  ←→  Staging Area (Index)  ←→  HEAD (Last Commit)
     |                    |                        |
  git diff           git diff --staged        (committed)
     |                    |                        |
     └────── git diff HEAD ──────┘
```

```bash
# Working tree vs staging area (unstaged changes)
git diff

# Staging area vs HEAD (what will be committed)
git diff --staged
git diff --cached  # same as --staged

# Working tree vs HEAD (all uncommitted changes)
git diff HEAD
```

## Comparing Commits and Branches

```bash
# Between two commits
git diff abc1234 def5678

# Between branches
git diff main feature-branch

# Changes introduced by a branch (since it diverged from main)
git diff main...feature-branch

# What changed in the last commit
git diff HEAD~1 HEAD
git show HEAD  # shortcut for last commit diff

# What changed in a specific commit
git show abc1234
```

## Scoping Diffs

```bash
# Specific file
git diff -- src/auth.js
git diff HEAD -- src/auth.js

# Specific directory
git diff -- src/

# Multiple paths
git diff -- src/auth.js src/session.js

# Exclude paths
git diff -- . ':!vendor/' ':!node_modules/'
```

## Output Formats

```bash
# Stat summary (files changed, insertions, deletions)
git diff --stat

# Name only (just file paths)
git diff --name-only

# Name with status (Added, Modified, Deleted)
git diff --name-status

# Word-level diff (instead of line-level)
git diff --word-diff

# Compact colored diff
git diff --color-words

# Generate a patch file
git diff > changes.patch
git diff main..feature > feature.patch

# Apply a patch
git apply changes.patch
```

## Reading Unified Diff Output

```diff
diff --git a/src/auth.js b/src/auth.js
index abc1234..def5678 100644
--- a/src/auth.js
+++ b/src/auth.js
@@ -10,7 +10,8 @@ function validateToken(token) {
   const decoded = jwt.verify(token, secret);
-  return decoded;
+  if (!decoded.exp || decoded.exp < Date.now() / 1000) {
+    throw new Error('Token expired');
+  }
+  return decoded;
 }
```

- `---` / `+++` — old and new file paths
- `@@` — hunk header showing line numbers
- `-` — line removed (red)
- `+` — line added (green)
- ` ` — context line (unchanged)

## Diff Options

```bash
# Ignore whitespace
git diff -w                 # ignore all whitespace
git diff --ignore-space-change  # ignore amount of whitespace
git diff -b                 # same as --ignore-space-change

# Show function name in hunk header
git diff -p  # default, shows function context

# Set context lines (default 3)
git diff -U5  # 5 lines of context
git diff -U0  # no context (just changes)

# Binary files
git diff --binary  # include binary diffs

# Check for problems
git diff --check  # warn about whitespace errors and conflict markers
```

## Agent Best Practices

1. **Always diff --staged before committing** — verify what you're about to commit
2. **Use --stat for overview** — see which files changed before diving into details
3. **Use -- path to scope** — focus on relevant files in large changesets
4. **Use --name-only for programmatic parsing** — easier to script
5. **Use triple-dot for branch comparison** — `main...feature` shows what the branch added
6. **Check for conflict markers** — `git diff --check` catches leftover markers

sections.title: Working tree, index, and HEAD diffs
sections.body: `git diff` compares working tree to index (unstaged changes). `git diff --staged` compares index to HEAD (what will be committed). `git diff HEAD` compares working tree to HEAD (all uncommitted changes). Understanding these three comparisons is fundamental — they correspond to the three areas in git's architecture. Always use `git diff --staged` before committing to verify intent.

sections.title: Comparing commits and branches
sections.body: Two-dot `git diff A..B` (or just `git diff A B`) compares the trees of two commits directly. Three-dot `git diff A...B` compares B against the merge base of A and B — this shows 'what did B add since diverging from A', which is what you want for branch comparison. `git show <commit>` is shorthand for the diff introduced by a single commit.

sections.title: Output formats and patch generation
sections.body: `--stat` for summary, `--name-only` for file list, `--name-status` for file list with add/modify/delete status, `--word-diff` for word-level changes, `--color-words` for inline color. Redirect to a file to create a patch: `git diff > file.patch`. Apply with `git apply file.patch`. Patches are the foundation of email-based git workflows.

sections.title: Reading unified diff format
sections.body: Lines starting with `-` were removed, `+` were added, ` ` (space) are context. The `@@` hunk header shows line numbers: `@@ -old_start,old_count +new_start,new_count @@`. The header after `@@` shows the enclosing function name. `diff --git a/path b/path` separates files. `index` line shows blob hashes and file mode.

related.note: Log shows commit metadata; diff shows content
Diff is essential for reviewing merge results

scripts.name: Smart Diff
scripts.description: Show diffs with smart defaults based on context
scripts.inline:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

MODE="${1:?Usage: smart-diff.sh <staged|unstaged|all|branch|commit> [args...]}"
shift

case "$MODE" in
  staged)
    echo "=== Staged changes (will be committed) ==="
    git diff --staged --stat
    echo
    git diff --staged
    ;;
  unstaged)
    echo "=== Unstaged changes ==="
    git diff --stat
    echo
    git diff
    ;;
  all)
    echo "=== All uncommitted changes ==="
    git diff HEAD --stat
    echo
    git diff HEAD
    ;;
  branch)
    TARGET="${1:?Target branch required (e.g. main)}"
    echo "=== Changes since diverging from $TARGET ==="
    git diff "$TARGET"...HEAD --stat
    echo
    git diff "$TARGET"...HEAD
    ;;
  commit)
    REF="${1:-HEAD}"
    echo "=== Changes in commit $REF ==="
    git show --stat "$REF"
    echo
    git show "$REF"
    ;;
  *)
    echo "Unknown mode: $MODE"
    echo "Usage: smart-diff.sh <staged|unstaged|all|branch|commit> [args...]"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

target.description: Branch or commit to compare against

paths.description: Files or directories to scope the diff

author: dojo-community
